edus.text: The 41st president of the United States , George Herbert Walker Bush , died on Friday , Nov. 30 at his Houston home .
He was 94-years-old .
As news of the World War II veteran 's death spread across the globe ,
family members , politicians and pop culture celebrities expressed gratitude
and paid tribute online to the life and legacy of the late president .
In a statement on Twitter , his grandson George P. Bush said
that H.W . 's most enduring legacy would n't be his public life , but the example
he set for his family .
`` He was more than a great man ;
he was a good man .
His courage was matched by his compassion ;
and his dedication to country was equaled only by his devotion to his family , ''
George P. Bush said .
`` Gampy , we love you
and we will miss you .
But we will never forget you . ''
The elder Bush was the son of a senator , Prescott Bush of Connecticut , and the father of Jeb Bush , the two-term governor of Florida , and George W. Bush , the two-term governor of Texas
who went on to win two terms as president .
Bush ,
who was 94 ,
died just months after his wife Barbara .
Early Saturday Jeb Bush tweeted ,
`` I already miss the greatest human being
that I will ever know .
Love you Dad ! ''
The 43rd president of the U.S. , George W. Bush , released a statement on behalf of himself and his siblings :
`` Jeb , Neil , Marvin , Doro and I are saddened to announce
that after 94 remarkable years , our dear Dad has died .
The entire Bush family is deeply grateful for 41 's life and love , for the compassion of those
who have cared
and prayed for Dad . ''
Former President Barack Obama said
America had lost a `` patriot and humble servant in George Herbert Walker Bush '' in a statement
released
after the news broke .
`` While our hearts are heavy today ,
they are also filled with gratitude .
Our thoughts are with the entire Bush family tonight -
and all
who were inspired by George and Barbara ' s example . ''
Despite a contentious public relationship ,
President Donald Trump also mourned the loss ,
saying in a White House statement
that `` through his essential authenticity , disarming wit , and unwavering commitment to faith , family , and country , President Bush inspired generations of his fellow Americans to public service
to be , in his words , ' a thousand points of light '
lluminating the greatness , hope , and opportunity of America to the world . ''
peaker of the House Paul Ryan issued a statement
aying
hat the elder Bush made the `` world safer and freer , ''
alling the former president a war hero .
` Like so many Americans , I admired President Bush not only for how he served
ut for how he lived , ''
yan said .
ormer President Jimmy Carter said
hat Bush 's administration was `` marked by grace , civility , and social conscience . ''
ormer Vice President Dan Quayle shared a story about the impact
ush had on his family
hile the two served in the White House :
` I have often told my children
f you want a role model in your life
ook to President George Herbert Walker Bush .
he world mourns the loss of a great American .
ut , it also celebrates a life
ell lived . ' ``
` What a life ... full of love of family ... strong faith ...
othing ' s more important .
od bless the Bush family at this sad time , ''
ormer vice presidential candidate Sarah Palin said .
hile they were foes on the campaign trail in the 1990 election ,
ormer President Bill Clinton worked with Bush
nce they were both out of office on different humanitarian efforts .
` He never stopped serving , ''
linton said in a joint statement with his wife , Hillary .
` I saw it up close ,
orking with him on tsunami relief in Asia and here at home after Hurricane Katrina .
is remarkable leadership and great heart were always on full display . "
ormer Vice President Joe Biden and his wife , Jill Biden , reflected on a time
hen Bush offered comfort to their family during a difficult time .
` When we went to Houston in 2015
o honor the doctors
hat cared for our deceased son Beau ,
resident Bush insisted on coming out
o see us . ''
hey called the elder Bush `` decent , kind , and welcoming , '' in a statement .
' Condoleezza Rice ,
ho served as secretary of state under Bush Junior ,
alled the senior president , `` the epitome of a public servant , '' in a statement on Facebook .
rom CEOs of major corporations and British royalty to pop culture figures
ho disagreed with Bush 's politics ,
igh profile world icons paid tribute to the former Republican president
hose public life spanned longer than 50 years .
` We lost a great American , ''
pple CEO Tim Cook said on Twitter .
ueen Elizabeth II reflected on her time with the man
he referred to as a `` great friend and ally of the United Kingdom . ''
n a statement Saturday , the queen referenced the late president 's military service during World War II .
` It was with sadness that I learned of the death of President George H W Bush last night .
rince Philip and I remember our days in Texas in 1991 with great fondness .
y thoughts and prayers are with President Bush 's family and the American people .
rnold Schwarzenegger took to Twitter
o offer condolences ,
eminding people to `` look up
nd offer him a silent thanks . ''
haz Bono , an actor and son of singer and actress Cher , said in a Tweet
hat
lthough he did n't agree with most of Bush 's policies ,
e appreciates
nd respects his dedication as president .
ther vocal liberal celebrities ,
ncluding Ellen DeGeneres and Andy Cohen ,
ook to social media
o pay their respects .
` I will never forget George H.W . Bush and President Clinton
eeting me in my old hometown of New Orleans
o show support
nd raise money after Hurricane Katrina , ''
llen tweeted .
ong before he was president ,
ervice was in his blood .
ush joined the Navy on his 18th birthday , months after the bombing of Pearl Harbor .
fter serving in World War II ,
e went to Yale
nd then moved his young family to Texas .
here , he found his fortune in both the oil industry and politics .
fter serving as the chairman of the Harris County Republican Party in Houston ,
e lost his first U.S. Senate bid .
e was then elected to the House of Representatives in 1966 ,
here he served for four years
efore losing another campaign for the Senate .
hat loss seemed to serve him fine , though .
resident Richard Nixon appointed Bush as the United Nations ambassador
nd then brought him on to lead the Republican National Committee during Watergate .
ollowing Nixon 's resignation ,
ush then named the chief of the U.S. Liaison Office in China by President Gerald Ford .
e then went on to become director of the Central Intelligence Agency .
ush became Ronald Reagan 's running mate in 1980 ,
erving as vice president for the next eight years .
hen he won the presidency in 1988 ,
e was ecstatic .
'We can now speak the most majestic words
democracy has to offer :
The people have spoken , ' ''
e said after his victory .
